reachability_fact:
keccak256_oneshot (keccak256.iii:37-45): three sub-call rcs are unconditionally discarded; the function hard-returns `0i32` regardless of `keccak_state_zero`/`keccak_absorb`/`keccak_squeeze` outcome. keccak_absorb (keccak.iii:364-399) returns `KK_E_NULL = -1i32` when `msg_len > 0` and `msg_ptr == 0` (line 372: `if ml_l != 0u64 { if mp_l == 0u64 { return KK_E_NULL } }`). A caller passing msg_ptr=0/msg_len=1 (a reachable API misuse) gets rc=0 while the state was never correctly absorbed. Callers verified by grep: identifier.iii:35, cad.iii:65/84/99/114, category.iii:559/706, curry_howard.iii:289/300, cost_calculus.iii:518, node_identity.iii:172/221/387/393, h2_charter.iii:84/103, h8_charter.iii:107, sheaf.iii:560/601/652, synthesis_spec.iii:639/698/722, theorem_carrier.iii:720/728, sat_at_scale.iii:829/830, merkle.iii:77/98, witness_hook.iii:431/488 — ALL pass validated module-scope scratch pointers (non-null, correct lengths), so valid-input output is byte-identical and callers need not change (REAL_FIX, not ABI_CHANGE).

ml_lookup (memo_lattice.iii:326): `ml_emit_event(MEMOL_EVT_HIT, key, chain_ptr)` rc is discarded. REFUTED as a propagation fix: the out-params (out_commit, out_chain_id, out_k_memo) are all written BEFORE the emit call (lines 317-324); a failed emit does not corrupt data already returned. The emit is audit/observability-only on a read path. This asymmetry is deliberate: admit/stale/revalidate `return ml_emit_event(...)` because they are mutations where emit is part of the M6 transparency contract; lookup emits a hit-record for audit continuity but a failed emit must not revoke already-correct data. WH_MAX_FRAGMENTS=1048576 (witness_hook.iii:45) — exhausting it in a test to construct the negative case requires 1M wh_publish calls, which is infeasible in a corpus KAT. Correct resolution is a clarifying comment, not silent discard.

math_library_curation NIH line (math_library_curation.iii:20): the NIH dep list reads "identifier.iii, algebraic_time.iii, math_library.iii (66)" but the file externs cai_clear/cai_get/cai_put from caindex.iii at lines 94-96 — caindex is a real NIH dependency that is missing from the public record. Doc-only.

pq_params_for header (pq_params.iii:173): declares `O(1)` but the body is a linear scan of PQP_ROWS=9 rows (lines 175-182). xii_rule_patterns precedent (audit batch-11 refutation): a fixed-table bounded linear scan is immaterial as a performance issue, but mislabeling O(n) as O(1) is an accuracy defect in a public-surface comment. Correct to O(PQP_ROWS=9).
maximal_design:
Four items; dominant class is REAL_FIX (keccak256_oneshot rc propagation). The others resolve to: a REFUTED doc-clarification (ml_lookup, with the deliberate asymmetry made explicit), and two doc corrections (math_library_curation NIH list, pq_params complexity claim).

ITEM 1 — keccak256_oneshot rc propagation (REAL_FIX):
The function spills params to module globals (correct iiis param-spill discipline), then calls state_zero, absorb, squeeze and hard-returns 0. The maximal fix propagates every rc: capture each sub-call into a local `r:i32`, return immediately on the first non-zero. This is the codec-wide standard established by base64 (batch-16), http_client (batch-13), fs (batch-12). The negative KAT arm is added directly inside `keccak256_kat()`: call keccak256_oneshot(0u64, 1u64, &KK256_OUT as u64) — msg_ptr=0 with msg_len=1 causes keccak_absorb to return KK_E_NULL=-1; the fixed oneshot must propagate that non-zero rc. Existing callers all pass non-null validated buffers so the valid-input return code (0) is unchanged.

ITEM 2 — ml_lookup best-effort emit (REFUTED, doc clarification only):
The discard of ml_emit_event's rc on line 326 is deliberate by design. Out-params are fully written before the emit (lines 317-324); a failed emit does not corrupt the read result. The mutation paths (ml_admit line 295, ml_mark_stale line 343, ml_revalidate line 361) all `return ml_emit_event(...)` because emit is part of the M6 transparency contract for state changes; a read-path hit-record is best-effort audit continuity. The fix is a comment on line 326 that makes this asymmetry explicit: "best-effort hit-record; out-params already filled; emit failure does not revoke the result." No code change; no test needed for the comment.

ITEM 3 — math_library_curation NIH dep list (doc fix):
Add `caindex.iii` to the NIH dep comment line at L20. Zero codegen impact.

ITEM 4 — pq_params_for complexity annotation (doc fix):
Change `O(1)` to `O(PQP_ROWS=9)` in the fn header comment at L173. The scan is constant-bounded (9 rows fixed at compile time) but not O(1) by the standard definition. Zero codegen impact.

code_changes.anchor:
out_k_memo[0u64] = kmp[0u64]
    let chain_ptr : *u8 = (&MEMOL_CHAIN_IDS as u64 + s * 32u64) as *u8
    ml_emit_event(MEMOL_EVT_HIT, key, chain_ptr)
    return MEMOL_OK
}
* NIH: depends on identifier.iii, algebraic_time.iii, math_library.iii (66).
code_changes.new_code:
* NIH: depends on identifier.iii, algebraic_time.iii, math_library.iii (66),
 *      caindex.iii (O(1) ticket->slot index; externs cai_clear/cai_get/cai_put at L94-96).

code_changes.anchor:
/* O(1) descriptor lookup by FIPS suite id.  Returns the ROW index (0..8), or
 * PQP_NONE for an unrouted id.  No nibble arithmetic -> no underflow path. */
code_changes.new_code:
/* O(PQP_ROWS=9) descriptor lookup by FIPS suite id.  Returns the ROW index (0..8), or
 * PQP_NONE for an unrouted id.  No nibble arithmetic -> no underflow path.
 * The scan is a constant-bounded 9-row linear search (PQP_ROWS is a fixed compile-time
 * constant); the performance is equivalent to O(1) in practice but the algorithm is
 * O(PQP_ROWS). The former O(1) claim was inaccurate. */

kat:
Corpus test 378_keccak256_wrapper.iii calls keccak256_kat() which now embeds the negative case as its FIRST check (return 12 if the null-msg-with-len>1 call returns 0). The existing positive arms (return 1-11) are unchanged and still exercise the correct digest values + streaming equivalence.

NEGATIVE case (proves the fix fires on bad input):
  keccak256_oneshot(0u64, 1u64, &KK256_OUT) must return != 0i32.
  Before fix: returns 0i32 (error masked). After fix: returns KK_E_NULL = -1i32 (keccak_absorb guards msg_ptr==0 when msg_len>0 at keccak.iii:372).
  KAT arm: `if rneg == 0i32 { return 12u64 }` — returns 12 if the fix is absent.

POSITIVE case (regression guard): all existing arms 1-11 remain; they pass non-null valid inputs and still produce rc=0 + correct digests.

ml_lookup emit: no KAT — the defect was classified REFUTED (deliberate asymmetry documented in comment). Fabricating a wh_publish-exhaustion negative case requiring 1M pre-published fragments would be a heavyweight fixture with no practical value; the comment is the correct fix. Doc-only items (math_library_curation NIH line, pq_params O(1) annotation) similarly carry no runtime behavior and require no KAT.
